message:
feat(engine): add frankenctl react doctor subcommand for mismatch catalog analysis

Implements `frankenctl react doctor --catalog <path>` which loads a
machine-readable React mismatch catalog, runs doctor/preflight analysis,
and emits a unified JSON report containing:

- Doctor report with per-domain findings
- Preflight pass/fail with blocker and advisory counts
- Support bundle with aggregate score
- Reproduction index with per-entry remediation status, advisory text,
  reproduction commands, and tags for operator triage

CLI surface:
- Parse --catalog (required), --out, --summary, --min-severity
  (info|warning|error|critical), --include-resolved, --target
  (nodejs|bun|deno|v8_reference, repeatable), --current-epoch,
  --trace-id, --decision-id, --policy-id
- Exit code 25 when preflight is blocked, 0 otherwise
- Help via `frankenctl react doctor --help` and `frankenctl react help doctor`
- Error messages updated from "compile|build|contract" to include "doctor"
- Command label dispatch refined from generic "react" to per-subcommand
  labels (react-compile, react-build, react-doctor, react-contract)
- React contract output now includes the doctor command entry

Tests (unit + integration):
- Parse test verifying all flags round-trip correctly
- Execution test constructing a mismatch catalog fixture, running
  execute_react_doctor, and asserting schema_version, blocked/ready state,
  repro index entry count, and entry_id
- Integration test via CARGO_BIN_EXE_frankenctl exercising the full CLI
  path and verifying stdout JSON matches the --out file
- React contract test now asserts "react doctor" appears in the commands array

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
